statements.sql:
-- Case 02 (UNSAFE): build an index without CONCURRENTLY.
-- Hazard: blocks all writes to the table for the entire build.
-- Expected findings: squawk require-concurrent-index-creation; eugene E6.
-- Catalog rewrite: postgres-catalog.md #4.
CREATE INDEX idx_orders_user_id ON orders (user_id)

triggered_rules.name: Creating a new index on an existing table
triggered_rules.condition: A new index was created on an existing table without the `CONCURRENTLY` keyword
triggered_rules.effect: This blocks all writes to the table while the index is being created
triggered_rules.workaround: Run `CREATE INDEX CONCURRENTLY` instead of `CREATE INDEX`
triggered_rules.help: Statement takes `ShareLock` on `public.orders`, blocking writes while creating index `public.idx_orders_user_id`

triggered_rules.name: Taking dangerous lock without timeout
triggered_rules.condition: A lock that would block many common operations was taken without a timeout
triggered_rules.effect: This can block all other operations on the table indefinitely if any other transaction holds a conflicting lock while `idle in transaction` or `active`
triggered_rules.workaround: Run `SET LOCAL lock_timeout = '2s';` before the statement and retry the migration if necessary
triggered_rules.help: Statement takes lock on `public.orders`, but does not set a lock timeout
